epics.title: Domain core: model + classic move rules (pure Swift, full TDD)

tasks.title: GameState model (tubes, balls, capacity, color count)
tasks.note: Tube/Move/GameState public value types (Equatable/Hashable/Sendable) in BallSortCore. Tube exposes top/isEmpty/isFull/count/isComplete; GameState holds tubes+capacity. Swift Testing suites green (swift test, 24 tests).

tasks.title: Legal-move validation (classic: empty or same-color top, not full)
tasks.note: GameState.isLegal(_:) enforces the classic rule (bounds, from!=to, non-empty source, non-full dest, dest empty OR top color match) + legalMoves() enumerates all legal moves. Full legality matrix covered by Swift Testing; swift test green.

tasks.title: Apply-move + win detection
tasks.note: GameState.apply(_:) is pure (returns new state or nil, never mutates self) and isWon = every tube empty-or-complete. Apply/win/purity covered by Swift Testing; swift test green.

tasks.title: Equality / hashing for search
tasks.note: GameState.canonical (GameState+Canonical.swift) sorts tubes into a deterministic total order so permutation-symmetric states collapse to one representative; synthesized Hashable/Equatable on the canonical form is the BFS/A* visited-set dedup key. swift test green.

tasks.title: Exhaustive unit tests
tasks.note: GameStateInvariantTests sweeps representative states x all legal moves: ball-multiset conservation, legality<=>apply, win soundness, and canonical properties (idempotent, permutation-stable, multiset/isWon preserving). swift test green.

epics.title: Level generator + solver
epics.summary: Guaranteed-solvable levels + difficulty grading. The hard algorithmic epic.

tasks.title: Solver (BFS/A*) → move sequence or nil
tasks.note: PR #3. Solving protocol + Solver struct (Solving.swift/Solver.swift). BFS over GameState, visited set keyed on .canonical (collapses tube-permutation symmetry → terminates without depth cap). Returns [] for already-won, a SHORTEST (min-moves) legal sequence for solvable, nil for unsolvable. Also powers hints (E6) and feeds E3.4 min-moves.

tasks.title: Generator (random-fill + solver-verification)
tasks.note: PR #4 (reverse-move scramble), REPLACED in PR #17. Reverse-move scrambling produced trivial ~2-move levels at any depth (random walk equilibrates near solved), so it was replaced with random-fill + solver verification (rejection sampling) tuned to a minMoves difficulty floor. New API generate(colors:capacity:emptyTubes:minMoves:using:); Generator takes a Solving; bounded to ≤5 colors (solver-feasible). SeededRandomNumberGenerator (SplitMix64) retained. See memory m11.

tasks.title: Solvability-guarantee test harness
tasks.note: PR #5. Test-only SolvabilityHarnessTests: parameter sweep colors 2…5 × capacity 3…4 × emptyTubes 1…2 × scrambleDepth {10,30,60} × 8 fixed seeds = 384 generated levels; each asserted structurally valid, Solver-solvable, and the solution replays to isWon. Seed+params in failure messages for reproducibility. Matrix capped colors≤5/capacity≤4 to keep BFS bounded.

tasks.title: Difficulty grading (colors, tubes, min-moves)
tasks.note: PR #6. DifficultyGrader + Difficulty type (Band trivial/easy/medium/hard/expert + numeric score; Sendable/Equatable/Comparable). grade(_:using: some Solving) — solver injected (DI). score = minMoves*3 + tubeCount + colorCount (monotonic in every dimension, strictly increasing in min-moves). Unsolvable → Difficulty.maximum. Feeds E5's rising curve.

tasks.note: Done — @Observable BoardViewModel (tap-lift/tap-drop, restart, isWon) delegating legality/apply to Core; XCTest unit-tested. PR #11.

tasks.title: Win overlay + next level

tasks.title: Difficulty-curve advancement
tasks.note: DifficultyCurve (App layer) drives generator-verified levels by a rising minMoves floor (PR #17); DifficultyBadgeView shows band (PR #21). NOTE: required fixing the E3 generator — reverse-move scramble produced trivial ~2-move levels; now random-fill + solver-verification (see memory m11).

tasks.title: Hint = solver next-best-move
tasks.note: Clean seam for future rewarded-ad sink.

tasks.title: Persist in-progress level
tasks.note: Foundation PRs #24 (Codable on Core) + #26 (PersistenceStore/JSONFileStore/InMemory fake). Integration: SavedGame envelope (level/gameState/initialState/moveCount/elapsed; undo history dropped) persisted by BoardViewModel.persistProgress() after every mutation/install; restored on launch via BoardViewModel(restoring:) wired in the composition root. Verified: real app writes saved-game.json to Application Support on launch.

tasks.title: Persist stats across relaunch
tasks.note: StatsStore (@Observable, App layer) loads GameStats from PersistenceStore on init and re-saves after each recorded win; BoardViewModel calls statsStore.recordWin(moves:seconds:) on win. Foundation: PR #25 (GameStats Core model) + #26 (store).

tasks.title: Stats + streaks model (levels solved, best moves/time, daily streak)
tasks.note: GameStats (Core, PR #25): levelsSolved, bestMoves, bestTimeSeconds, currentStreak, longestStreak, lastSolvedDay (yyyymmdd key). recordingWin(moves:seconds:day:) is pure; daily-streak adjacency uses Foundation Calendar (correct across month/year/leap). StatsStore.currentDayKey() supplies today's local-calendar key.

tasks.title: Stats screen

tasks.title: SFX (drop / click / win)

tasks.title: Haptics (lift / drop / complete)

epics.title: Level history, replay & appearance toggle

tasks.title: Appearance toggle (System/Light/Dark)
tasks.note: AppearanceMode (system/light/dark) persisted under @AppStorage("appearanceMode"); SettingsView segmented picker; composition root applies .preferredColorScheme app-wide (incl. sheets). Zen tokens already carry light+dark, so no color refactor. Defaults to System. Advances E12.16. TDD: AppearanceModeTests + SettingsViewTests.

tasks.title: Per-level run history model + store
tasks.note: Core LevelRun/LevelHistory value types (run = level, moves, time, yyyymmdd day-key, + the level's STARTING board snapshot for exact replay; newest-first, capped 200). App HistoryStore (@Observable) persists under "history" key. BoardViewModel records a run on win. Wins-only (per Tom). Full TDD.

tasks.title: History screen (Summary/History tabs)
tasks.note: RunHistoryView (dumb, Zen) lists runs newest-first with moves/time/date + Retry. StatsScreen gains a Summary/History segmented control. Behavioral tests (StatsScreenTests) + #Previews; OS-fragile Zen sheet not snapshot-tested per memory.

tasks.title: Retry a specific level (side excursion)
tasks.note: BoardViewModel.replay(_:)/exitReplay(): install a past run's EXACT board as a side excursion; current level stashed + persistProgress suppressed so relaunch resumes the real level; exit restores it. Replay win sharpens records (GameStats.improvingBests) but does NOT advance the curve or inflate levelsSolved/streak (practice, per Tom). RootView replay banner + Exit; win overlay primary action becomes Done while replaying. Full TDD (BoardViewModelReplayTests).

epics.title: Gameplay depth — feel + guidance
epics.summary: Deepen the core loop without a twist mechanic: richer game-feel (pour-arc, drag, win/completion payoff) plus difficulty & guidance fixes (curve plateau, first-run tutorial, smarter hints). Tom-requested 2026-06-27, scope-expansion accepted (launch can slip to get gameplay right). Extends E8 (juice), E6 (hints), E3 (difficulty); not a new rule, so GAME_RULES out-of-scope list is unchanged.

tasks.title: Fix difficulty-curve plateau (Core)
tasks.note: B1. DifficultyCurve currently flatlines by ~level 9: colors capped at 5, empty tubes floored at 1, capacity fixed at 4, min-moves capped at 24 — contradicts PROJECT_BRIEF 'infinite rising difficulty'. Grow capacity (4->5->6) and raise color/min-move ceilings on a continued curve while keeping every level solvable (Generator rejection-sampling + solver). Pure Core/App-curve work, full TDD. Recommended FIRST task. Watch solver feasibility cap (grader limited to 5 colors). SHIPPED: capacity grows 4->5->6 (L11/L16) gated behind the 1-empty-tube transition; min-moves ceiling 24->48; colors stay <=5. Difficulty potential rises through L20 (was flat from L8). Probe found solver cost scales with empty-tube count, not board size. 160 app tests green; capacity-6 board renders cleanly. Branch feat/e14.1-difficulty-curve-plateau.

tasks.title: First-run interactive tutorial
tasks.note: B2. No onboarding exists today — players land cold on L1. Add first-run detection (@AppStorage) + a short interactive walkthrough of the classic move rule (lift top ball; drop on empty or same-color non-full). Skippable, replayable from Settings. Behavioral VM tests + screenshot per UI rule. Biggest early-churn reducer for the store launch. SHIPPED: TutorialViewModel (@Observable, pure, fully unit-tested) drives a 3-step skippable/replayable walkthrough; TutorialOverlayView reuses ZenOverlayCard + pill buttons + step dots; @AppStorage(hasSeenTutorial) gate in RootView; Settings 'How to Play' replays it. 171 app tests green, lint clean, 2 snapshot baselines. Branch feat/e14.2-first-run-tutorial.

tasks.title: Pour-arc move animation
tasks.note: A1. Today a moved ball springs straight to the destination (BoardView dropAnimation). Replace with a real arc travelling from source tube mouth to destination mouth — the signature ball-sort feel. Keep existing lift spring + tube-complete flourish. Snapshot the primitive + device recording. SHIPPED: flying-ball overlay arcs from the source ball's actual slot to the landing slot (PourGeometry.topBallPoint, pure+unit-tested; PourArcEffect GeometryEffect for the true parabola; lastMove VM seam; dest top ball suppressed during flight). Verified good on device (Tom); launch-position bug fixed. 181 tests green.

tasks.title: Drag-to-pour gesture
tasks.note: A2. Tap-to-lift/tap-to-drop is the only interaction (BoardView/TubeView). Add a drag gesture (drag a source tube onto a destination) alongside taps, sharing the same legality + feedback path. Pairs with E14.3. Reject with the existing shake. Device recording required. IMPLEMENTED (TDD): extracted a private BoardViewModel.commitMove(_:next:) from tap()'s drop branch (existing tap tests unchanged), and added a self-contained pour(from:to:) intent both routes share — identical legality/feedback/pour-arc seam. A board-level DragGesture in BoardView (minimumDistance 12, board coordinate space) hit-tests source/dest off the already-captured tubeRects: first movement lifts the tube under the finger via tap() (lift haptic + selection glow + legal targets highlight), release over another tube pours, release on source/gap/empty cancels. nearestTube(toX:) gives a forgiving single-row drop. 7 new pour VM tests; full app suite 188 green; lint clean. Verified good on device (Tom) — taps and drags coexist, feel is clean. DONE.

tasks.title: Win-moment particle payoff
tasks.note: A3. WinOverlayView is just a staggered card cascade today. Add a particle/confetti burst behind the card so victory lands. Respect reduce-motion. Snapshot static frame + recording. IMPLEMENTED: WinParticleBurst view — a calm 22-dot bloom in the 6-stone river-stone palette that drifts outward from centre with a gentle gravity sag and fades once (AnimationConstants.winBurst, 0.9s ease-out), layered behind the win card in RootView's win branch (plays for real + replay wins). Deterministic (per-index angle/reach/size/colour, no randomness) so it's snapshot-stable; `settled` freezes a mid-bloom frame. Honours Reduce Motion by rendering nothing (card entrance carries the moment). Snapshot baseline recorded (WinParticleBurst.bloom.png); full app suite 182 green; lint clean. PENDING: device feel check by Tom (live burst on a real win).

tasks.title: Tube-completion settle polish
tasks.note: A4. Tube completion currently = scale pulse + moss glow + two-note tone (TubeView/BoardView). Add a tactile 'lock/settle' finish on the final ball (small overshoot + ripple) to make completion feel decisive. Extends E8.

tasks.title: Smarter hints + hint feedback
tasks.note: B3. Hints are highlight-only and the hint event is silent (no sound/haptic). Add an animated nudge toward source/dest and wire a hint GameEvent into the feedback service. Keep solver-derived (E6). VM tests for the new event.

tasks.title: Difficulty label on level intro
tasks.note: B4. DELIVERED BY E12.7. DifficultyBadgeView already renders 'Level N · Band' persistently atop the game screen (RootView.swift), color-coded along a calm trivial→expert ramp; BoardViewModel.difficultyBand serves the exact solver-derived grade once computed, falling back to curve.estimatedBand(forLevel:) meanwhile. The HUD-surfacing goal of B4 is satisfied. A distinct level-intro *announcement* moment (calling out the band as you enter a level) was considered and deferred — not worth a separate overlay; the persistent badge covers the 'gauge the climb' intent. No new code this task.
